stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results April 20: PAT +35% YoY to Rs 2,014 cr (crossed Rs 2,000 cr milestone for first time ever); FY26 PAT Rs 7,019 cr (+27% YoY). NII Q4 +18.81% YoY to Rs 3,702 cr. Advances +21.74% YoY to Rs 2.92 lakh cr; deposits +14.14% to Rs 3.51 lakh cr; total business +17.47% to Rs 6.43 lakh cr. Gross NPA improved to 1.45% (-29 bps YoY); Net NPA best-in-class 0.13%. Retail loans +32% YoY. CRAR 18.36%, CET1 14.59%. Final dividend Rs 1.20/share declared on top of interim Rs 1 (total FY26 Rs 2.20). Board approved Rs 7,500 cr equity raise + Rs 10,000 cr infrastructure bonds for FY27. Stock hit 52-wk high Rs 81.17 on April 21 (+5.46% day, +22% in one month); closed around Rs 79. HDFC Securities 'Buy' maintained; Mojo Score upgraded to 'Strong Buy' (84).
stocks.sector_context: Nifty PSU Bank index up ~15% in April 2026 vs Nifty 50's 9.6% - a sector of the month. Strong Q4 results across lenders (SBI, BOB, BOI, Canara, Union Bank all gained up to 4%). Credit growth (~13.5%) outpacing deposit growth (~11%), creating sector-wide NIM pressure. MAHABANK clearly at the top of the PSU pack on growth and asset quality.
stocks.peer_comparison: Bank of Maharashtra stands out as arguably the strongest PSU performer - 35% PAT growth and 0.13% Net NPA place it in a different league vs peers battling margin compression. 21.74% advances growth outpaces SBI, BOB, Canara. P/B premium vs smaller PSUs now justified by execution. Retail/RAM-led model driving outperformance.
red_flags: Stock at 52-wk high Rs 81.17 after +22% one-month rally - stretched near-term entry point, profit-booking risk
Rs 7,500 cr equity raise announcement creates near-term dilution overhang (depending on pricing/timing)
Sector-wide NIM pressure as funding costs rise and deposit competition intensifies
Low-priced PSU stock (~Rs 79) - can be volatile on FII flows
catalysts: Record date for final dividend Rs 1.20/share (post-AGM)
Capital raise execution (Rs 7,500 cr QIP) - near-term watchpoint
Continued PSU Bank index momentum (+15% April)
Best-in-class asset quality supports multiple re-rating

stocks.recent_news: MAJOR NEGATIVE: Stock crashed ~9-10% on April 22 to ~Rs 1,302 - top Nifty loser, worst session in 11 years. Q4 FY26 results April 21: PAT +4.2% to Rs 4,488 cr (below street); revenue +12.34% YoY to Rs 33,981 cr but Q4 revenue of $3,682M -3.3% QoQ (missed consensus -1.6%). Net new deal wins $1.9 bn -35% YoY. EBIT margin 16.5% vs 17.6% consensus. FY27 revenue guidance slashed to 1-4% constant currency (vs FY26's 4-4.5%) - well below Street's 3-5% expectations. Root causes cited: budget cuts at US telecom clients, SAP project cancellations, restrained client spending. Interim dividend Rs 24/share declared, record date April 25, payment May 5. HSBC 'Hold' TP cut to Rs 1,480 (from Rs 1,560); JPMorgan 'Neutral' TP cut to Rs 1,370 (from Rs 1,419); MOFSL retains 'Buy'. Mcap slipped below ITC and 4 others post-crash.
stocks.sector_context: Nifty IT index under heavy pressure on April 22 as HCLTECH selloff drags sector. TCS (April 9) and Wipro (April 16) already reported muted prints; Tech Mahindra April 22 and Infosys April 23 imminent. Tier-1 FY27 guidance tone decisively cautious - demand environment weak, US telecom/BFSI budgets under pressure. Weak INR provides some cushion but not enough to offset demand weakness. Sector FY27 revenue growth expectations now being trimmed. Nifty IT worst-performing sector in April.
stocks.peer_comparison: HCLTECH now the weakest among Tier-1 prints - Q4 miss + weakest FY27 guidance among peers. Infosys (3-6% expected guide) and TCS likely to fare better. Product seasonality and Verticals business hit HCLTECH disproportionately. Persistent Systems and mid-caps outperforming Tier-1s. MOFSL prefers Persistent over HCLTECH near-term.
red_flags: Stock down 9-10% intraday April 22 - worst session in 11 years, acute near-term momentum breakdown
FY27 guidance cut to 1-4% CC - well below consensus, signals prolonged demand weakness
Net new deal wins -35% YoY - lead indicator for FY27 revenues deteriorating
EBIT margin 16.5% vs 17.6% consensus - operating leverage broken
US telecom client budget cuts + SAP project cancellations = structural client-concentration risk
Analyst TP cuts underway (HSBC, JPMorgan) - more downgrades likely post-call
catalysts: Interim dividend Rs 24/share record date April 25, payout May 5 - near-term liquidity event
Oversold technical bounce possible after -10% panic move
MOFSL and select brokers retain Buy - value-based bottom-fishing potential
Infosys Q4 (April 23) read-across - if peer also disappoints, sector de-rated; if peer beats, HCLTECH relative discount widens further

stocks.recent_news: Stock Rs 161.45 on April 21 (up from Rs 156.55 on April 20); 52-wk range Rs 134.36-Rs 202.79; Mcap ~Rs 1.03 lakh cr; P/E 13.38. Q4 FY26 board meet scheduled May 7, 2026. Q3 FY26 was weak: PAT Rs 1,603 cr (down from Rs 2,217 cr QoQ), PBT Rs 2,030 cr vs Rs 2,823 cr QoQ - 57% YoY profit plunge on margin pressure. Multiple pipeline project delays announced: KKBMPL pushed to Sept 2026, Mumbai-Nagpur-Jharsuguda to June 30 2026, JHBDPL Dhamra-Haldia to Sept 2026. Capex reallocation to renewables: Rs 3,800 cr for 700 MW solar+BESS in UP/Maharashtra (April 15) and Rs 3,294.86 cr for 600 MW solar + 550 MWh BESS in UP (April 13). Government plans to list GAIL Gas subsidiary in FY28 (~Rs 31 bn).
stocks.sector_context: Oil & gas Q4 FY26 outlook weak: Brent +24.2% QoQ, spot LNG $13.2/mmbtu (vs $10.9 Q3), Henry Hub $4.9/mmbtu, INR -2.8% QoQ. Gas transmission volumes forecast -6.7% YoY / -12.1% QoQ for Q4. Systematix expects CGD margin compression across IGL/MGL/Gujarat Gas. Broker views divided: Systematix 'Hold' TP Rs 176, Emkay 'Add' 12-29% upside, Kotak 'Sell'. Middle East/US-Iran escalation two-way risk - supports LNG prices but hurts CGD economics.
stocks.peer_comparison: GAIL's diversified transmission+petchem+LPG model provides buffer vs pure-play CGD peers (IGL/MGL/Gujarat Gas) facing APM gas allocation cuts. P/E 13.38 and Mcap Rs 1.03 lakh cr make it the largest, most liquid name in Indian gas utility space. 52-wk drawdown from Rs 202.79 to ~Rs 161 (-20%) is deeper than many peers. Trades at 5-9% discount to pre-Iran-war levels.
red_flags: Q3 FY26 PAT -57% YoY - sustained margin deterioration trend
Multiple pipeline project delays (KKBMPL/MNJPL/JHBDPL) pushing revenue recognition out
Rs 7,100+ cr solar/BESS capex commitments pressure near-term FCF and ROCE
Q4 FY26 setup weak: transmission volumes -6.7% YoY, higher LNG cost, INR weakness
Kotak 'Sell' rating adds to bearish analyst mix
catalysts: Q4 FY26 board meet May 7, 2026 - binary event
APM gas allocation policy decision from government
Middle East de-escalation would ease LNG cost pressure
Pipeline commissioning updates (KKBMPL/MNJPL)
GAIL Gas subsidiary IPO narrative (FY28)

stocks.recent_news: Stock ~Rs 1,534 on April 20, 2026; Mcap ~Rs 20,650-20,932 cr; P/E 39.3. 1-month return +10.3%, 1-year -21.5%, -36% from 52-wk high. April 17: company confirmed NOT a Large Corporate under SEBI debt framework. April 3: Board reappointed Maulik Mehta, Meghav Mehta, Sanjay Upadhyay and two independent directors. April 1: Commissioner (Appeals) upheld GST order against subsidiary DPL - recovery Rs 2.15 cr + penalty Rs 2.15 cr (immaterial). Subsidiary Deepak Chem Tech commissioned nitration+second hydrogenation plant at Dahej on Jan 19, 2026 (~Rs 85 cr capex). Q3 FY26 was weak: net profit +1.73% to Rs 99.79 cr, sales +3.76% to Rs 1,974.97 cr. Next earnings date likely May 2026.
stocks.sector_context: Specialty chemicals sector pressured in early April 2026: Nifty Chemicals -3.44% to 24,899.80 after government waived import duties on petrochemical products - SRF -5.53%, Navin Fluorine -4.79%, PI Industries -3.54%, Aarti Industries -3.05% on April 2. Petrochem duty waivers hurt Indian derivatives margins. Longer-term supportive: China's removal of export-tax rebates effective April 2026 could tighten global supply and support margins for Indian chemical makers over 12-24 months. IDBI Capital 'Buy' on SRF TP Rs 3,517 and Navin Fluorine TP Rs 8,265.
stocks.peer_comparison: 1-month +10.3% is modest bounce but 1-year -21.5% places Deepak among weaker specialty-chemical performers. Core phenolics business (DPL) faces persistent phenol/acetone spread pressure; structural growth narrative lags fluorine peers (SRF +30.7% 1Y, Navin Fluorine +33.8% 1Y). Trades at discount to SRF/PI/Navin on EV/EBITDA reflecting commoditised phenolics exposure.
red_flags: -21.5% one-year return signals sustained structural underperformance
Sector-wide hit from India's petrochem import-duty waiver in early April 2026
Phenol/acetone spreads remain under pressure - commoditised exposure
Q4 FY26 results date not officially confirmed - information vacuum
Structural re-rating lag vs fluorine-focused peers
catalysts: Q4 FY26 results expected May 2026
Dividend announcement
China export-rebate removal benefit from April 2026 onwards
Deepak Chem Tech Dahej plant ramp-up (nitration + hydrogenation)
1-month momentum (+10.3%) suggests bottoming attempt

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results announced April 15: Consolidated net profit -96% YoY to just Rs 6 cr (from Rs 146.48 cr), hit by non-cash Rs 101.77 cr goodwill impairment + tax anomaly. Revenue -6.51% YoY to Rs 745.61 cr (vs Rs 797.57 cr). FY26 annual standalone PAT was Rs 476.70 cr vs Rs 340.03 cr in FY25, so underlying business strong; revenue from operations +6.2% YoY to Rs 2,366.05 cr. Order intake Rs 657 cr in Q4; open order book Rs 1,292 cr as of March 31. Divisional split: Material Handling Equipment (MHE) revenue +36.8% YoY to Rs 274 cr, but Gear Division revenue declined. Appointed Chintan Shah as CFO effective April 15. Final dividend Rs 1.5/share recommended. Stock rose ~3.3% on result day to ~Rs 428.50 (investors looked past headline loss). Stock Rs 419.5 on April 10; 52-wk range Rs 352-Rs 716.25 (still ~41% below 52-wk high). Management reaffirmed FY27 guidance.
stocks.sector_context: Capital goods / industrial machinery sector mixed in April 2026. Engineering names with overseas exposure facing tariff-uncertainty tailwind/headwind. Gear/power transmission segment facing demand variability; MHE & bulk-material-handling strong on India infra capex. Peers L&T, ABB, Siemens, Cummins benefiting from domestic infra theme; small/mid-cap industrials diverging on execution.
stocks.peer_comparison: ELECON's -41% drawdown from 52-wk high Rs 716 is steeper than most industrial peers. MHE division growth (+36.8%) in line with sector infra tailwind; but Gear Division weakness (core business) is a relative negative vs peers with cleaner order books. Q4 EPS effectively wiped out by goodwill impairment whereas peers reporting clean Q4s. Order book Rs 1,292 cr provides some FY27 visibility. Stock trades at discount to large-cap industrials on past underperformance.
red_flags: Q4 FY26 PAT -96% YoY (Rs 6 cr) - though non-cash goodwill impairment, optically a shock print
Gear Division revenue declining - a structural concern for core business
Stock -41% from 52-wk high Rs 716 - sustained weakness
Revenue -6.51% YoY in Q4 signals top-line softness beyond just goodwill hit
Order intake Rs 657 cr in Q4 is modest given Rs 1,292 cr open book - watch book-to-bill trajectory
catalysts: Management reaffirmed FY27 guidance - continuity signal
New CFO (Chintan Shah) effective April 15 - leadership refresh
MHE Division +36.8% YoY growth momentum
Final dividend Rs 1.5/share
Goodwill impairment is non-cash and one-off - clean comps from Q1 FY27
Order book Rs 1,292 cr + early overseas recovery expected per CMD
